title: Pastebin
description: Design a text paste sharing service that allows users to create, store, and share text snippets with unique URLs

coreRequirements.label: Paste Creation
coreRequirements.description: Users can submit text content and receive a unique shareable URL.
examplePhrases: Submit text content to create a new paste.
System generates a unique URL for sharing.

coreRequirements.label: Paste Retrieval
coreRequirements.description: Access paste content via the unique URL or ID.
examplePhrases: Anyone with the URL can view the paste.
Retrieve paste content by its unique ID.

coreRequirements.label: Unique Identifiers
coreRequirements.description: Each paste has a unique ID that maps to exactly one piece of content.
examplePhrases: Every paste gets a unique identifier.
No two pastes share the same ID.

optionalRequirements.label: Paste Expiration
optionalRequirements.description: Support optional expiration dates or TTL after which pastes are deleted.
examplePhrases: Pastes can expire after a set period.
Support TTL for temporary pastes.

optionalRequirements.label: Syntax Highlighting
optionalRequirements.description: Detect programming language and apply syntax highlighting.
examplePhrases: Auto-detect language for syntax highlighting.
Support code formatting for various languages.

optionalRequirements.label: Visibility Options
examplePhrases: Users can set pastes as public, unlisted, or private.
Control who can access the paste.

optionalRequirements.label: User Accounts
optionalRequirements.description: Support user accounts to manage owned pastes.
examplePhrases: Users can sign in to manage their pastes.
Track paste ownership for registered users.

optionalRequirements.label: Raw Content Access
optionalRequirements.description: Provide raw text endpoint for CLI tools and embedding.
examplePhrases: Access raw content via /raw/:id endpoint.
Support curl and CLI tools for raw access.

coreRequirements.label: Performance and Latency
coreRequirements.description: Paste retrieval should be fast. Aim for sub 150ms p95 on reads.
examplePhrases: Reads should target p95 under 150ms.
Keep response times low for good UX.

coreRequirements.label: Scalability and Throughput
coreRequirements.description: Handle growing traffic and content volume.
examplePhrases: Support millions of pastes.
Handle thousands of requests per second.

coreRequirements.label: Availability
examplePhrases: Target 99.9% availability.
Avoid single points of failure.

optionalRequirements.label: Storage Efficiency
optionalRequirements.description: Optimize storage costs for large text content.
examplePhrases: Use object storage for cost-effective content storage.
Compress content to reduce storage costs.

optionalRequirements.label: Caching Strategy
examplePhrases: Cache popular pastes at the edge.
Use CDN for global distribution.

optionalRequirements.label: Security
optionalRequirements.description: Protect against abuse and malicious content.
examplePhrases: Rate limit paste creation.
Scan content for malicious patterns.

coreRequirements.label: Paste Creation Endpoint
coreRequirements.description: POST endpoint to create new pastes with documented request/response

coreRequirements.label: Paste Retrieval Endpoint
coreRequirements.description: GET endpoint to retrieve paste content

optionalRequirements.label: Raw Content Endpoint
optionalRequirements.description: GET endpoint for raw text content
examplePhrases: GET /raw/:id returns plain text content

optionalRequirements.label: Delete Endpoint
examplePhrases: DELETE /api/v1/pastes/:id requires authentication

optionalRequirements.label: Error Handling
examplePhrases: 404 for not found, 410 for expired pastes

requiredEndpoints.purpose: Create a new paste from text content
requiredEndpoints.exampleNotes: POST with body containing content, optional title, and expiresAt. Returns id and url.
GET returns paste content and metadata. 404 if not found, 410 if expired.

optionalEndpoints.purpose: Get raw text content without formatting
optionalEndpoints.exampleNotes: Returns plain text content for CLI tools.
DELETE requires authentication. Returns 204 on success.

feedbackTemplates.missing: How will HTTP requests from users reach your backend? What component handles routing and rate limiting?
feedbackTemplates.present: Good! Your API Gateway routes incoming requests.

feedbackTemplates.missing: What handles the core logic for creating and retrieving pastes?
feedbackTemplates.present: Good! Service component handles paste operations.

feedbackTemplates.missing: Where will you store large text content cost-effectively? Think about blob storage for unstructured data.
feedbackTemplates.present: Excellent! Object storage is ideal for paste content.
Great! CDN caches popular pastes at the edge for low latency.
Good! Database stores paste metadata for efficient querying.

criticalPaths.name: Basic Data Flow
criticalPaths.description: Core path from API Gateway through Service to Object Store

feedbackTemplates.missing: Connect API Gateway → Service → Object Store to complete the data flow.
feedbackTemplates.present: Perfect! Your data flow is complete.

feedbackTemplates.missing: How does API Gateway route to your Service?
feedbackTemplates.present: Good! API Gateway correctly routes to services.
feedbackTemplates.incorrect: API Gateway should route to Service, not directly to storage.

feedbackTemplates.missing: How does Service persist paste content?
feedbackTemplates.present: Great! Service can store content in Object Store.
Excellent! CDN caches content from Object Store for fast delivery.
